hypothesis: In molecular systems with three or more metastable basins, iteratively applying ASTRA between neighboring basins (A→I1, I1→I2, …, In→B) will reliably locate the first-order saddle points for each elementary step—yielding sampled configurations whose committor values from short unbiased dynamics are centered at 0.5—whereas applying ASTRA directly between nonadjacent basins will not produce committor-centered TS ensembles.
